source.title: Delay discounting and intelligence: A meta-analysis

confidence_rationale: Meta-analysis of 24 studies (26 effect sizes) with r = -0.23 is a robust, replicable finding. The mapping to ARC-026 is indirect: lower delay discounting is a necessary but not sufficient condition for long-horizon benefit detection. The paper measures preference structure, not approach-gradient sensitivity specifically. Transfer risk is moderate because delay discounting involves explicit monetary choice tasks, not the graded spatial approach manifolds ARC-026 posits.
failure_signatures: Effect size r = -0.23 is small-to-medium; substantial variance unexplained by intelligence
Yeh et al. 2021 replication with HCP sample (n=1206) found relationship weakened after controlling for SES
Delay discounting measures explicit choice preference, not implicit approach-gradient learning
Laboratory monetary tasks may not generalize to naturalistic long-horizon care investment

mapping.source_claim_statement: Higher intelligence (g) is associated with lower delay discounting (r = -0.23 across 24 studies); individuals with greater cognitive ability prefer larger delayed rewards over smaller immediate ones.
mapping.ree_translation: ARC-026 predicts that benefit-gradient sensitivity (approach toward distant goods) scales with agent capability faster than contact-detection (binary harm avoidance). Reduced temporal discounting in intelligent agents is precisely what this predicts: a more linear or less steeply discounted value function allows graded future benefit signals to guide behavior. If love = sustained approach investment toward a distant beneficiary, then the cognitive substrate for love-like behavior (low discounting, valuation of future states) grows with intelligence.
mapping.mapping_caveat: Delay discounting is a proxy for one dimension of long-horizon valuation. ARC-026 specifically contrasts approach-gradient learning (graded, spatially distributed benefit signal) with harm avoidance (binary contact signal). The meta-analysis does not decompose by signal type. The REE translation assumes that long-horizon benefit integration is the cognitive operation linking g to lower discounting.
mapping.source_context: Behavioral economics / differential psychology. Well-replicated paradigm (real and hypothetical monetary intertemporal choice). Intelligence measured via IQ-proxy tasks. Cross-sectional human adult samples.

confidence_components.notes: Source quality high (meta-analysis, no publication bias detected). Mapping fidelity moderate: discounting =/= approach gradient sensitivity but is the closest available empirical proxy. Transfer risk moderate: monetary choice paradigms abstract away from relational/spatial gradient learning.
